summary: notifier-utils@1.3.7 fetches and executes remote JavaScript via jsonkeeper.com, impersonating pino logger
details: The package impersonates the pino logger (cloned file layout under lib/pino, meta.js version '9.6.0', keywords 'fast, logger, stream, json', and an exported alias module.exports.pino = middleware) to lure consumers into invoking its middleware. When the exported middleware is called, index.js spawns `node lib/caller.js` as a detached, stdio-ignored child process and calls child.unref() to survive the parent's exit and suppress output. lib/caller.js then performs `axios.get` against https://jsonkeeper.com/b/BPB86, extracts the response body, and executes it via `new Function.constructor("require", src)(require)`, retrying up to 5 times. `require` is passed into the constructed function, granting the fetched code full Node privileges on the consumer host. lib/const.js additionally holds a base64-encoded backup endpoint that decodes to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J with header name 'x-secret-key', concealing the fallback loader from static inspection. The remote source is a mutable public paste with no pinning, no integrity check, and no signature — the author can swap in arbitrary payloads at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
